title: Cerastium adamovicii
taxonomy: Cerastium adamovicii is classified as a species within the family Caryophyllaceae. This family comprises a large number of flowering plants commonly known as the pink or carnation family. Cerastium adamovicii is further classified as a member of the genus Cerastium, which includes several species of annual or perennial plants. The genus Cerastium is often referred to as the mouse-ear or chickweed family, due to the appearance of its leaves and stems. Cerastium adamovicii is native to certain regions of Europe and Asia, and its classification reflects its botanical relationships with other plants in these areas.
